cards.title: Valid Contract (Act of 1872)
cards.content:
ΓÇó Needs Offer & Acceptance, Lawful Consideration, and Free Consent.
ΓÇó Capacity: Minors or people of unsound mind cannot enter contracts.
ΓÇó Consensus Ad-Idem: Agreeing on the same thing in the same sense.

cards.title: Utmost Good Faith (Uberrima Fides)
cards.content:
ΓÇó Both parties must declare all "Material Facts".
ΓÇó Differs from normal commercial contracts which run on "Caveat Emptor" (Let the buyer beware).

items.text: You must suffer a financial loss if the insured event happens. No interest = Gambling.
The nearest, most direct, and active cause that led to the loss.
Restores you to the same financial state. (NOTE: Indemnity does NOT apply to Life Insurance).

sections.title: ≡ƒÆí Exam Master Tips
items: Material Fact: Any fact that would influence the underwriter's decision to accept the risk or fix the premium.
Breach of Utmost Good Faith makes the contract VOIDABLE at the option of the insurer.
Misrepresentation is giving incorrect information innocently. Fraud is doing it intentionally.
Contract of Adhesion: Insurance policies are "take it or leave it". You cannot negotiate the printed terms.
In Life Insurance, Insurable Interest must exist ONLY at the time of taking the policy, not necessarily at the time of death.
